summary: Verified CHILD review mode for completed quest. Logged in as classmate_g3, opened Quests page, clicked completed 'Media Test Quest' card. Modal opened successfully showing: (1) large general quest image (naturalWidth=512), (2) 'View PDF' link pointing to /api/uploads/quests/9fbcb5516779.pdf, (3) Questions section with Question 1 image (naturalWidth=912). Both images visually render (naturalWidth > 0). Card is clickable (cursor=pointer, no disabled attribute).

integration_issues.issue: Frontend login form submits username-based logins to /api/auth/school-login which returns 401 for classroom child 'classmate_g3'. The working endpoint is /api/auth/login with {identifier, password}. This blocks any test/QA of school child accounts through the UI. Bypassed in this test by calling /api/auth/login directly to establish session cookie.

issues: Console warning: Missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent (a11y warning, non-blocking)

action_items: Fix login page: route username-only logins (classmate_g3 etc.) to /api/auth/login with identifier field, or fix /api/auth/school-login to accept these classroom children credentials. Currently only email-based accounts work via UI.
critical_code_review_comments: QuestsPage.jsx correctly opens completed quests in review mode; image and PDF rendering works end-to-end.
Login flow has a routing mismatch between frontend school-login handler and backend expectations.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The child-side quest review mode is fully working: completed quest card is clickable, opens modal with quest image, View PDF button, and per-question image. Separate pre-existing issue: /login UI cannot authenticate classmate_g3-style school users because it hits /api/auth/school-login which returns 401; use /api/auth/login with identifier to bypass. Not caused by current change but worth flagging.
rca of the issue: Task fix (making completed quest cards clickable) is verified correct. openQuest sets selectedQuest without needing an active session; modal renders image_url, pdf_url and per-question images correctly.
